question: US x China tariff agreement by May 31st? [Polymarket]
reason: Recent US-China trade talks have led to tariff reductions and agreements on agricultural imports.
evidence: On May 12, 2026, the US and China announced a trade truce, reducing US tariffs on Chinese goods from approximately 145% to 30% and Chinese tariffs on US goods from 125% to 10%. ([abhs.in](https://www.abhs.in/blog/us-china-trade-truce-beijing-summit-chip-export-controls-semiconductor-may-2026?utm_source=openai))

Following a summit in Beijing on May 14-15, 2026, China agreed to purchase at least $17 billion worth of US agricultural products annually through 2028, including beef and poultry. ([apnews.com](https://apnews.com/article/832bafb5ca0be21e4a1d149c5db56b58?utm_source=openai))
